# MSBuild Item Management Patterns

Canonical patterns for working with item groups, from `Microsoft.Common.CurrentVersion.targets`.

## Include / Remove / Update — Three Operations

| Operation | Purpose | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| `Include` | Add new items to the group | Creating items with identity + metadata |
| `Remove` | Remove items matching a pattern | Excluding files or clearing a group |
| `Update` | Modify metadata on existing items | Adding/changing metadata without re-adding |

### Include — Add Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="Generated\*.cs">
    <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
  </Compile>
</ItemGroup>
```

### Remove — Subtract Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Remove specific items -->
  <Reference Remove="$(AdditionalExplicitAssemblyReferences)" />

  <!-- Set subtraction: prior minus current -->
  <_CleanOrphanFileWrites Include="@(_CleanPriorFileWrites)"
      Exclude="@(_CleanCurrentFileWrites)" />

  <!-- Clear an entire group -->
  <_Temporary Remove="@(_Temporary)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Update — Modify Existing Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <EmbeddedResource Update="@(EmbeddedResource)"
      Condition="'%(NuGetPackageId)' == 'Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections'">
    <GenerateSource>true</GenerateSource>
    <ClassName>Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections.SR</ClassName>
  </EmbeddedResource>
</ItemGroup>
```

`Update` does not add items — it only modifies items already in the group.

## Item Batching — %(Metadata)

When `%(Metadata)` appears in target attributes or task parameters, MSBuild **batches** execution per unique metadata value.

### Target-level batching (Outputs)

```xml
<Target Name="GenerateSatelliteAssemblies"
    Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(_SatelliteAssemblyResourceInputs)"
    Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(Culture)\$(TargetName).resources.dll">
  <!-- Runs once per unique Culture value -->
</Target>
```

### Task-level batching

```xml
<Copy SourceFiles="@(_SourceItems)"
    DestinationFiles="@(_SourceItems->'$(OutDir)%(TargetPath)')">
</Copy>
```

### Per-item filtering with Condition

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <_ResxOutput Include="@(EmbeddedResource->'%(OutputResource)')"
      Condition="'%(EmbeddedResource.WithCulture)' == 'false'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Batching rules

- `%(Metadata)` in `Condition` or `Outputs` → target batches per unique value.
- `%(Metadata)` in task parameters → task batches per unique value.
- **Do not mix `%()` from different item groups** in the same expression — this causes a cross-product (see Common Pitfalls).

## Item Transforms — @(Item->'expression')

Transforms create new item lists by applying an expression to each item:

```xml
<!-- Transform file paths to destinations -->
<Copy SourceFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly)"
    DestinationFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly->'$(OutDir)%(Filename)%(Extension)')"/>

<!-- Transform with separator for display -->
<Message Text="Files: @(Compile->'%(Filename)', ', ')" />
```

## Exclude Pattern — Set Subtraction on Include

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="**\*.cs" Exclude="Generated\**;Tests\**" />
</ItemGroup>
```

`Exclude` only works on `Include` — it cannot be used with `Update` or `Remove`.

## Conditional Item Inclusion

```xml
<!-- Condition on ItemGroup — all or nothing -->
<ItemGroup Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'">
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines" />
</ItemGroup>

<!-- Condition on individual items -->
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines"
      Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## PrivateAssets on Tool/Analyzer Packages

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Update="Microsoft.NETCore.App" PrivateAssets="All" />
  <PackageReference Include="StyleCop.Analyzers" PrivateAssets="all" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## Common Pitfalls

### Cross-product batching

Referencing `%(Metadata)` from two different item groups creates O(N×M) executions:

```xml
<!-- BAD: Cross-product of @(Source) × @(Config) -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with %(Config.Identity)" />

<!-- GOOD: Reference one group via batching, the other via property -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with $(ConfigFile)" />
```

### Generated files in source tree

Write to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` (obj/), not the source directory. Source-tree generation pollutes version control and can cause duplicate compilation via globs.

### Missing FileWrites

Every file created during a target must be added to `@(FileWrites)` for `dotnet clean` support.

Canonical patterns for working with item groups, from `Microsoft.Common.CurrentVersion.targets`.

## Include / Remove / Update — Three Operations

| Operation | Purpose | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| `Include` | Add new items to the group | Creating items with identity + metadata |
| `Remove` | Remove items matching a pattern | Excluding files or clearing a group |
| `Update` | Modify metadata on existing items | Adding/changing metadata without re-adding |

### Include — Add Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="Generated\*.cs">
    <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
  </Compile>
</ItemGroup>
```

### Remove — Subtract Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Remove specific items -->
  <Reference Remove="$(AdditionalExplicitAssemblyReferences)" />

  <!-- Set subtraction: prior minus current -->
  <_CleanOrphanFileWrites Include="@(_CleanPriorFileWrites)"
      Exclude="@(_CleanCurrentFileWrites)" />

  <!-- Clear an entire group -->
  <_Temporary Remove="@(_Temporary)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Update — Modify Existing Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <EmbeddedResource Update="@(EmbeddedResource)"
      Condition="'%(NuGetPackageId)' == 'Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections'">
    <GenerateSource>true</GenerateSource>
    <ClassName>Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections.SR</ClassName>
  </EmbeddedResource>
</ItemGroup>
```

`Update` does not add items — it only modifies items already in the group.

## Item Batching — %(Metadata)

When `%(Metadata)` appears in target attributes or task parameters, MSBuild **batches** execution per unique metadata value.

### Target-level batching (Outputs)

```xml
<Target Name="GenerateSatelliteAssemblies"
    Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(_SatelliteAssemblyResourceInputs)"
    Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(Culture)\$(TargetName).resources.dll">
  <!-- Runs once per unique Culture value -->
</Target>
```

### Task-level batching

```xml
<Copy SourceFiles="@(_SourceItems)"
    DestinationFiles="@(_SourceItems->'$(OutDir)%(TargetPath)')">
</Copy>
```

### Per-item filtering with Condition

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <_ResxOutput Include="@(EmbeddedResource->'%(OutputResource)')"
      Condition="'%(EmbeddedResource.WithCulture)' == 'false'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Batching rules

- `%(Metadata)` in `Condition` or `Outputs` → target batches per unique value.
- `%(Metadata)` in task parameters → task batches per unique value.
- **Do not mix `%()` from different item groups** in the same expression — this causes a cross-product (see Common Pitfalls).

## Item Transforms — @(Item->'expression')

Transforms create new item lists by applying an expression to each item:

```xml
<!-- Transform file paths to destinations -->
<Copy SourceFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly)"
    DestinationFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly->'$(OutDir)%(Filename)%(Extension)')"/>

<!-- Transform with separator for display -->
<Message Text="Files: @(Compile->'%(Filename)', ', ')" />
```

## Exclude Pattern — Set Subtraction on Include

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="**\*.cs" Exclude="Generated\**;Tests\**" />
</ItemGroup>
```

`Exclude` only works on `Include` — it cannot be used with `Update` or `Remove`.

## Conditional Item Inclusion

```xml
<!-- Condition on ItemGroup — all or nothing -->
<ItemGroup Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'">
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines" />
</ItemGroup>

<!-- Condition on individual items -->
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines"
      Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## PrivateAssets on Tool/Analyzer Packages

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Update="Microsoft.NETCore.App" PrivateAssets="All" />
  <PackageReference Include="StyleCop.Analyzers" PrivateAssets="all" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## Common Pitfalls

### Cross-product batching

Referencing `%(Metadata)` from two different item groups creates O(N×M) executions:

```xml
<!-- BAD: Cross-product of @(Source) × @(Config) -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with %(Config.Identity)" />

<!-- GOOD: Reference one group via batching, the other via property -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with $(ConfigFile)" />
```

### Generated files in source tree

Write to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` (obj/), not the source directory. Source-tree generation pollutes version control and can cause duplicate compilation via globs.

### Missing FileWrites

Every file created during a target must be added to `@(FileWrites)` for `dotnet clean` support.

data.reasoningText:
Let me understand all the bugs:

1. **BUG 1 (line 16-21)**: `Compile Include="**\*.g.cs"` duplicates items because SDK already globs `*.cs`. Should use `Update` instead.

2. **BUG 2 (line 36-38)**: Inside the batched target, `Compile Include` and missing `FileWrites`. The ItemGroup inside the target with `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` should work for Compile, but FileWrites is missing.

3. **BUG 3 (line 46-48)**: FileWrites in a static ItemGroup at evaluation time - the transform works but it's evaluated before the target runs. Actually wait, the transform `@(ProtoFile->'...')` would work at evaluation time since ProtoFile items exist. But the issue is that FileWrites should be registered inside the target, not outside.


4. **BUG 4 (line 56-61)**: Cross-product batching with both `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` and `%(ValidationRule.Filename)` causes 3×2=6 executions instead of 3.

5. **BUG 5 (line 67-71)**: `Remove="**\*.g.cs"` is too broad, should be scoped to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)`.

Now I need to fix these issues. For the first one, I should change `Include` to `Update` for the generated files since they're in the obj directory, not the source tree.

Actually, wait—I'm realizing the real issue here. The SDK already globs `**/*.cs` by default, which includes `**/*.g.cs` files. So adding another explicit `Include="**\*.g.cs"` creates duplicate items in the compilation. The generated files go to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)`, and if there are any .g.cs files in the source tree, they'd be picked up twice—once by the SDK's default glob and once by this explicit include.

The fix is to change `Include` to `Update` on line 17, which just sets metadata on the existing items instead of creating duplicates. That eliminates the CS2002 warnings.

For the validation target issue where it's running 6 times instead of 3, the problem is cross-product batching. I need to batch only on ProtoFile and handle the ValidationRule items differently to avoid the multiplication effect. Then I'll need to fix the DependentUpon metadata handling since `Update` works differently than `Include`, and clean up the static FileWrites ItemGroup and the CleanGeneratedCode Remove pattern.

Now I'm checking whether the Update pattern would actually match anything. The generated files are in the obj/ directory, not the source tree, so `Update="**\*.g.cs"` wouldn't find them unless there are already .g.cs files checked in. Looking at the directory listing, there's a `Constants.g.cs` at the root, so the pattern might match some existing files in the source tree.

The real issue is that the SDK's default Compile glob already excludes obj/ and bin/, so an explicit Include wouldn't create duplicates from the generated files. But I should focus on what the comments actually suggest: switching from Include to Update for the metadata-setting Item.

For the validation batching, I need to run the validation once per proto file rather than creating a cross-product with all rules. I can batch only on ProtoFile and reference the ValidationRule items as a transformed list so each proto file gets validated against all rules in a single pass.

For the FileWrites fix...

Implementing the fixes now...

data.content:
All 5 bugs fixed:

1. **CS2002 duplicate warnings** → Changed `Compile Include` to `Compile Update` (line 15) so metadata is set on SDK-globbed items without re-adding them.
2. **Clean doesn't remove generated files** → Added `FileWrites` inside the batched target (line 36) so all 3 generated files are tracked.
3. **Stale static FileWrites** → Removed the `ItemGroup` outside the target that evaluated too early; registration now happens correctly inside the target.
4. **Validation runs 6× instead of 3×** → Eliminated cross-product batching by using `@(ValidationRule->'%(Filename)', ', ')` transform instead of `%(ValidationRule.Filename)`, so the task batches only on `ProtoFile` (3 iterations).
5. **Over-broad clean** → Scoped `Remove` from `**\*.g.cs` to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)*.g.cs` so only generated files in `obj/` are removed.
